ci_support_linux_64_.yaml:
c_stdlib:
- sysroot
c_stdlib_version:
- '2.17'
channel_sources:
- conda-forge
channel_targets:
- conda-forge main
cxx_compiler:
- gxx
cxx_compiler_version:
- '14'
docker_image:
- quay.io/condaforge/linux-anvil-x86_64:alma9
eigen_abi_devel:
- 3.4.0
- 5.0.1
libboost_devel:
- '1.88'
libboost_python_devel:
- '1.88'
numpy:
- '2'
pin_run_as_build:
  python:
    min_pin: x.x
    max_pin: x.x
python:
- 3.10.* *_cpython
- 3.11.* *_cpython
- 3.12.* *_cpython
- 3.13.* *_cp313
- 3.14.* *_cp314
target_platform:
- linux-64

about.summary: A template-based Library for creating curves of arbitrary order and dimension, eventually subject to derivative constraints. The main use of the library is the creation of end-effector trajectories for legged robots.

name: ndcurves

about.summary: A template-based Library for creating curves of arbitrary order and dimension, eventually subject to derivative constraints. The main use of the library is the creation of end-effector trajectories for legged robots.

A template-based Library for creating curves of arbitrary order and dimension, eventually subject to derivative constraints. The main use of the library is the creation of end-effector trajectories for legged robots.

raw_meta_yaml:
schema_version: 1

context:
  name: ndcurves
  cppname: lib${{ name }}
  pythonname: ${{ name }}-python
  version: "2.1.1"

recipe:
  name: ${{ name }}-split
  version: ${{ version }}

source:
  url: https://github.com/loco-3d/${{ name }}/releases/download/v${{ version }}/${{ name }}-${{ version }}.tar.gz
  sha256: 43682331a49ed26772269a847181c7fc92bd9a12ed8de2edf2ba3c7080ccbaa0
  patches:
    - patches/0001-PATCH-cmake-Don-t-link-with-all-pinocchio.patch

build:
  number: 0

outputs:
  - package:
      name: ${{ cppname }}
    build:
      script:
        - if: unix
          then: build_ndcurves.sh
          else: build_ndcurves.bat
    requirements:
      build:
        - if: build_platform != target_platform
          then:
            - python
            - cross-python_${{ target_platform }}
            - numpy
        - cmake
        - ninja
        - ${{ compiler('cxx') }}
        - ${{ stdlib("c") }}
      host:
        - libboost-devel
        - eigen
        - eigen-abi-devel
        # Because pinocchio 3.9 still depends on 'hpp-fcl'
        - libcoal
        - libpinocchio
        # Because pinocchio CMake module needs Python
        - if: win and python_version >= "3.10"
          then: numpy >=2.1
          else: numpy
      run_exports:
        - ${{ pin_subpackage(cppname, upper_bound='x.x.x') }}
    tests:
      - package_contents:
          include:
            - ndcurves/fwd.h

  - package:
      name: ${{ pythonname }}
    build:
      script:
        - if: unix
          then: build_ndcurves_python.sh
          else: build_ndcurves_python.bat
    requirements:
      build:
        - if: build_platform != target_platform
          then:
            - python
            - cross-python_${{ target_platform }}
            - numpy
        - cmake
        - ninja
        - ${{ compiler('cxx') }}
        - ${{ stdlib("c") }}
      host:
        - libboost-python-devel
        - eigenpy
        # Because pinocchio-python 3.9 still depends on 'hpp-fcl'
        - coal
        - pinocchio
        - ${{ pin_subpackage(cppname, exact=True) }}
        - if: win and python_version >= "3.10"
          then: numpy >=2.1
          else: numpy
      run:
        - python
        - coal
        - pinocchio
        - if: win and python_version >= "3.10"
          then: numpy >=2.1
          else: numpy
    tests:
      - python:
          imports:
            - ndcurves
          pip_check: false

  # Metapackage
  - package:
      name: ${{ name }}
    requirements:
      host:
        # Explicitly add a python requirement to change hash between python versions
        - python
      run:
        - ${{ pin_subpackage(cppname, exact=true) }}
        - ${{ pin_subpackage(pythonname, exact=true) }}
      run_exports:
        - ${{ pin_subpackage(cppname, exact=true) }}

about:
  homepage: https://github.com/loco-3d/ndcurves
  repository: https://github.com/loco-3d/ndcurves
  summary: >
    A template-based Library for creating curves of arbitrary order and dimension,
    eventually subject to derivative constraints.
    The main use of the library is the creation of end-effector trajectories for legged robots.
  license: BSD-2-Clause
  license_file: LICENSE

extra:
  feedstock-name: ${{ name }}
  recipe-maintainers:
    - ManifoldFR
    - jorisv

about.summary: A template-based Library for creating curves of arbitrary order and dimension, eventually subject to derivative constraints. The main use of the library is the creation of end-effector trajectories for legged robots.
